the hexagonal community center has six equilateral triangles as its faces, each with side length 30 meters. the area of one triangle is calculated using the formula (sqrt(3)/4) * side², so the total area of the hexagon is six times this value. the radius of the circular garden is equal to the distance from the center of the hexagon to the midpoint of one of its sides, which is also the apothem of the hexagon. the apothem can be calculated using the formula (s * sqrt(3))/2, where s is the side length. the area of the circular garden is then calculated using the formula π * r².  
the circular garden has a circumference that touches the midpoints of the hexagon's sides, so the radius is the same as the apothem. the area of the circular garden is π * (s * sqrt(3)/2)². the total area of the hexagon is six times (sqrt(3)/4) * 30². the radius of the circular garden is 30 * sqrt(3)/2, and the area of the circular garden is π * (30 * sqrt(3)/2)².
